fact: Return is rolling out a 'virtual battery' concept, allowing energy companies to access a network of battery projects rather than contracting capacity at a single site, as reported in the October 2025 APG investment announcement (ESG Today, October 20, 2025; APG press release, October 16, 2025).

Return is unique in operating independently at scale, without trading energy itself. — APG asset management publication, October 16 2025 (https://assetmanagement.apg.nl/publications/apg-invests-300-million-in-return-s-battery-energy-storage-platform/), verbatim bullet in the article summary by APG Senior Investment Director Bart Saenen. Corroborated by Return's own live homepage (https://return.energy): 'Return provides storage infrastructure; traders decide how to use the capacity. This enables traders to expand portfolios without investment or operational burden, while Return remains fully independent.'
Prior to this transaction, Return had already invested a similar amount in its core operations. (return.energy press release, October 2025)

Dutch market leader SemperPower will now operate under the Return brand. The full integration was announced February 4, 2025, with complete brand transition confirmed by April 2, 2025. As of today (July 1, 2026), Return continues to operate under this unified brand, with no trace of the SemperPower name on the current return.energy homepage.

Return has acquired a majority stake in Hamburg-based J&P Batterie Projekte GmbH with a €50 mln investment and commitment. (Source: return.energy/news/2024-09-19, announced 19 September 2024; corroborated by J&P's own news page at jp-batterie.com, Silicon Canals, Mercom Capital, Tamarindo, and Crunchbase — all dated September 2024.)

Dutch developer Lion Storage (part of Return) secured an irrevocable building permit for its 364 MW / 1,457 MWh Project Mufasa BESS located in Vlissingen port, Netherlands — announced June 2024. The project subsequently reached full financial close (February 2025) and is listed as 'fully funded' on Return's live Locations page (return.energy/locations).
